prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: LOD 2 full Japan railway atlas plate
Primary request: Create a polished early-2000s Japanese transport simulator overview of the whole Japanese archipelago, slightly closer than a national map, with city clusters, mountain ranges, bays, coastline, forested islands and subtle rail corridor hints.
Style/medium: A-Train / SimCity BuildIt inspired isometric pixel-art, dense miniature details, readable map, not photorealistic.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9, actual Japan silhouette and island placement, no UI, no labels, no readable text.
Constraints: preserve real Japan geography; no giant trains, no fantasy islands, no logos, no watermark.

Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: LOD 4 central Honshu railway region plate
Primary request: Create a polished isometric pixel-art central Honshu railway region, closer than nationwide: Tokyo Bay, Izu coast, Shizuoka/Fuji area, Ise Bay, Nagoya, Kyoto/Osaka, mountain corridors and dense urban clusters.
Style/medium: late-1990s/early-2000s Japanese rail management sim, detailed map texture, cohesive miniature world.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 with Tokaido corridor implied left-to-right, no UI, no labels, no readable text.
Constraints: must feel geographically central Japan, not generic fantasy terrain; no logos, no watermark.

Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: LOD 6 Tokyo-Yokohama rail approach plate
Primary request: Create a cohesive isometric pixel-art urban railway region between Tokyo and Shin-Yokohama: dense city fabric, waterways, highways, rail yards, viaducts, parks, station approaches and small trains.
Style/medium: early-2000s Japanese transport management sim, crisp readable pixel detail, not photorealistic.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9, diagonal railway flow through the frame, no UI, no labels, no readable text.
Constraints: must feel like real Tokyo/Kanagawa urban corridor; no giant trains, no logos, no watermark.

Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: LOD 8 close Tokyo Station district plate
Primary request: Create a detailed isometric pixel-art Tokyo Station rail district, closer than a city overview: platform roofs, many parallel tracks, shinkansen trains, overhead gantries, Marunouchi/Yaesu office blocks, roads, trees, canals and tiny passengers.
Style/medium: polished late-1990s Japanese railway management game, dense hand-painted pixels, cohesive production game art.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 no UI, no labels, no readable text.
Constraints: central Tokyo station vibe, no rural fields, no logos, no watermark.

Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: LOD 10 close Shin-Yokohama district plate
Primary request: Create a detailed isometric pixel-art Shin-Yokohama station district close view: elevated Shinkansen platforms, local roads, dense mid-rise blocks, river/canal hints, parks, station plaza, overhead wires, small cars and tiny people.
Style/medium: early-2000s Japanese transport sim, detailed cohesive miniature city.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 no UI, no labels, no readable text.
Constraints: Yokohama urban rail district, not countryside or fantasy coast; no logos, no watermark.

Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: LOD 12 close Nagoya Station district plate
Primary request: Create a detailed isometric pixel-art Nagoya Station close rail scene: terminal platforms, broad roads, surrounding commercial towers, rail approaches, overhead gantries, station plazas, subtle Nagoya civic/castle cues, trees and vehicles.
Style/medium: polished early-2000s Japanese transport management sim, dense readable pixel-art.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 no UI, no labels, no readable text.
Constraints: must feel like Nagoya, not generic Tokyo; no logos, no watermark.

Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: LOD 14 close Kyoto Station district plate
Primary request: Create a detailed isometric pixel-art Kyoto Station close rail scene: Shinkansen platforms, gridded streets, compact city blocks, temple roof accents, mountain edge hints, rivers/canals, warm wood tones, catenary, tiny vehicles and passengers.
Style/medium: early-2000s Japanese railway management sim, cohesive dense pixel-art.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 no UI, no labels, no readable text.
Constraints: must feel like Kyoto urban station district; no rural-only scene, no logos, no watermark.

Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: LOD 16 close Shin-Osaka station district plate
Primary request: Create a highly detailed isometric pixel-art Shin-Osaka rail interchange close scene: station roofs, many Shinkansen tracks, local lines, roads, canals/rivers, dense Osaka urban blocks, overpasses, catenary, small vehicles and people.
Style/medium: polished late-1990s/early-2000s Japanese railway management game, dense cohesive pixel art like a finished game screen.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 no UI, no labels, no readable text.
Constraints: Shin-Osaka urban rail district; no logos, no watermark, no giant foreground train.
